findings_none: Every re-executed check is green on the candidate tree, the mirror-before-dispatch pin is present and mutation-proven enforced on all six plan-run surfaces, the end-to-end band walk found no cycle-critical un-machine-enforced behavior cut, the two removed validator pins fossilized only a deleted Band-3 heading, no provenance token entered any of the 12 surfaces, and the opencode/kimi reds are byte-for-byte identical at base and after.

## Review narrative

What I read first: the canonical review context at kaola-workflow/issue-725/.cache/review-contexts/a8e4326cca71d94dadf1b25e6a7ebd65cc481e8b83462411f320482de1a38c1b.json (confirmed: validation_obligations is empty, review_phase discovery, no prior findings, no repair delta), then n1's evidence at kaola-workflow/issue-725/.cache/n1-routing-diet.md (nonce fc2d5a8c0215 echoed above), the issue #725 Phase D three-band rule, and the frozen n1/n2 briefs in kaola-workflow/issue-725/workflow-plan.md. I isolated the n1 slice of the lane-group merge (leg commit a5832727 vs its base): exactly the two skeletons, templates/routing/required-blocks.js, the 12 generated surfaces, the two forge validators, and n1's own evidence file — matching the frozen write set; the agents/ and adaptive-node.js deltas in the merge belong to the n7/n9 legs and are outside this gate.

Re-execution (all from the worktree, observed outputs): generate-routing-surfaces --check exit 0 ("all 12 surfaces byte-match the skeleton"); validate-workflow-contracts.js exit 0; validate-kaola-workflow-contracts.js exit 0; gitlab and gitea contract validators exit 0; test-route-reachability.js exit 0 ("2277 assertions"); cmp of scripts/validate-workflow-contracts.js vs plugins/kaola-workflow/scripts/validate-workflow-contracts.js exit 0 (byte-identical); simulate-workflow-walkthrough.js exit 0 (this is the KEEP-rule oracle asserting ACTIVE_WORKTREE_PATH and Working directory against the trimmed plan-run command surface). For the seeded run gap I re-verified base-vs-after myself in a scratch worktree at 1491c7e5: test-opencode-edition.js fails with exactly the same single failure at base and at HEAD (H1 #F3 hookPath, 385 passing both sides) and test-kimi-edition.js FATALs identically both sides (ENOENT on the Phase-C-retired hooks/kaola-workflow-pre-commit.sh, referenced from scripts/sync-kimi-edition.js, outside n1's write set). n1's diff introduced no new failure in either suite, consistent with the seeded context fact; the gate claim does not assert those suites green.

Mirror-before-dispatch: the PIN marker and the exact "Apply the returned taskTransitions to the visible task list BEFORE" line are present on all six plan-run surfaces (grep-verified per file), placed at the step-3 dispatch seam next to the every-spawn-parameter-comes-from-the-card discipline. The new pr-mirror-before-dispatch manifest block in templates/routing/required-blocks.js follows conventions (topic plan-run, both/both tags, marker-first content tokens whose interior tokens are not substrings of the marker). I proved enforcement, not just presence, with a mutation probe in a scratch worktree at the candidate commit: altering one token of the mirror line on a single surface (the gitea plan-run SKILL) turned test-route-reachability.js red with a MANIFEST derived-universe failure (2 failures, 2276 passed). The unchanged 2277 assertion total vs base is consistent: the manifest presence check is one aggregate assert whose obligatedCount grew by 6 (scripts/test-route-reachability.js:1290), so n1's "+6 obligated file-checks" phrasing is accurate.

Pin narrowing: the only validator edits are two removed assertBefore(..., '### Co-active Folders Advisory', '## Routing') lines (gitlab validator ~line 448, gitea ~455), which pinned solely the Band-3 advisory heading the diet merged into ## Co-active Folders; all surrounding load-bearing pins (KAOLA_REASONING=, target_unverified, the Startup refusal template, the roadmap offline path, Startup Step 0a-1, the Branch: status template) are untouched, and the root/codex validators, test-route-reachability.js, slots.js, and the opencode/kimi suites were not modified — nothing was deleted wholesale.

Band-misclassification walk-through: I read every hunk of both skeleton diffs and traced the execution flows in the trimmed surfaces. Plan-run: the diet touched only two sections — the scripts-path gotcha (compressed, re-resolve-per-call rule and hardcode option kept) and Validation De-Duplication (compressed; the un-machine-enforced Band-1 kernel — reuse-boundary honesty, run-chains once at All-done via the ## Meta validation_command — kept; the hash-staleness consequence is Band-2, machine-enforced by the plan-validator's typed final_validation_unbound/final_validation_stale refusals at scripts/kaola-workflow-plan-validator.js:119). The full node lifecycle is intact and untouched: orient, open-next, dispatch (cards, teammate mode, reviewer contract envelope), close-and-advance, all-done, with speculation (7 mentions), write-halt (6), reconcile-running-set (5), open-ready (10), close-node (6), repair/reopen (26/5), consent (19), leg isolation (33), and .cache/ evidence paths (16) all present on the rendered command surface — an orchestrator can still run a full node lifecycle, speculation, and halt/repair routing from what remains. Next: every cut keeps its typed-refusal token or mechanic — bundle lane keeps bundle_requires_adaptive, target_ambiguity, the exact env/flag forms and KAOLA_BUNDLE_MAX_ISSUES default; Path Intent keeps the path_not_installed single-authority rule (the dropped replan_in_progress fence sentence is Band-2: machine-enforced refusals at scripts/kaola-workflow-claim.js:2770, scripts/kaola-workflow-adaptive-node.js:111, scripts/kaola-workflow-adaptive-handoff.js:65, and a fence mention survives on the rendered surfaces via slots at commands/workflow-next.md:22); Git Freshness keeps the ff-only rule, the stop conditions, and the verbatim release-with---reason git-freshness-block recipe; Resume Detection keeps the validity test and the three-way reconstruction routing table; State Bootstrap keeps the conservative-repair fields and do-not-fabricate conditions; the startup verdict routing (owned/acquired/no_target/claim "none") and refusal-classification (result: refuse vs result: escalate) blocks survive with their pins. No cycle-critical un-machine-enforced behavior was cut as narration.

Provenance: an enumeration of every #-token across all 12 surfaces yields only markdown headings and the #N runtime placeholder (filed: #N); no #NNN issue refs, ADR ids, or decision IDs entered any prompt surface.

Miss-reason honesty (AC-D judgment): both recorded reasons are honest. next.skeleton at 894 lines profiles as 208 code-fence lines (validator-pinned startup bash, issue-scout YAML, status templates), 56 template markers, 185 blanks, 35 headings, and only 410 prose lines — reaching ≤450 requires removing ~444 lines, which is arithmetically impossible by prose diet alone and confirms the residual mass is the machine-pinned apparatus plus the structural command-vs-skill duplication (the section map shows the full startup/bundle/routing apparatus rendered twice, lines ~102-505 and ~508-894), exactly the shared-SLOT refactor n1 declined; the residual safely-cuttable Band-2/3 prose I could find (a duplicated scout-justification parenthetical, a stale fast-summary.md mention at skeleton line 120 — pre-existing, not candidate-caused) totals a few dozen lines at most, nowhere near the gap. plan-run.skeleton's recorded reason is likewise accurate: outside the manifest-pinned mechanic blocks, the only prose-heavy cuttable sections were the two the diet already cut, and the #718 line adds mass, so the ~766 floor stands well above 400.

review_attestation: full_review_completed
review_conclusion: The n1 routing diet is a faithful Band-2/3 compression with no misclassified Band-1 cut, its mirror-before-dispatch pin is present and mutation-proven enforced on all six plan-run surfaces, the narrowed pins removed only a deleted-heading fossil, all re-executed gates are green with the opencode/kimi reds unchanged from base, and both recorded line-target miss reasons are arithmetically honest, so this gate approves the candidate.
